title: pilosella caespitosa subsp. redenicensis
intro: Pilosella caespitosa subsp. redenicensis is a subspecies of the plant Pilosella caespitosa, commonly known as red-stemmed mouse-ear hawkweed. It is a perennial plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Pyrenees. The medicinal properties of Pilosella caespitosa subsp. redenicensis include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its health benefits.
